page_title: Valid passports and other travel documents needed to come to Canada - Canada.ca
h1: Valid passports and other travel documents needed to come to Canada

h2: How long your passport needs to be valid
Keep 1 page in your passport free
Documents we don’t accept (unreliable travel documents)

sections.heading: How long your passport needs to be valid
items: Check the expiry date on your passport. Depending on the program you’re applying for, you may want to renew your passport before you submit your application.

sections.heading: Visiting, working and studying temporarily
items: We can’t issue you a work permit, study permit or visitor record that is valid past the expiry date of your passport.
If you get a new passport after you arrive in Canada, you can apply to extend your stay.

sections.heading: Immigrating through Express Entry
items: If you’re applying through Express Entry, you should renew your passport if it expires within 6 months from the day you apply for permanent residence . Then, update your new passport information in your application. This will ensure it’s valid when we process your application.

sections.heading: Keep 1 page in your passport free
items: If you’re from a visa-required country , you need at least 1 free page in your passport or travel document. That’s where we’ll stick your visitor visa or permanent resident visa.

sections.heading: Documents we don’t accept (unreliable travel documents)
items: The following documents are considered unreliable and are not valid for travel to Canada:
bullets: non-machine readable passports issued by the Czech Republic
temporary passports issued by the Republic of South Africa
